LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS THOMAS NGUYEN, DIEP LE, AND SAIGON VILLAGE, LLC MOTION TO CHANGE VENUE FROM SAN FRANCISCO SUPERIOR COURT TO TO ALAMEDA COUNTY SUPERIOR COURT, STATE OF CALIFORNIA. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED AND MODIFIED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: MOTION DENIED AS TO DEFENDANTS THOMAS NGUYEN AND DIEP LE ONLY. THE MAIN RELIEF SOUGHT IS FOR RECOVERY OF THE FULL AMOUNT OWED UNDER THE LOAN AGREEMENT. THE CASE ARISES FROM A UNDERLYING BREACH OF CONTRACT. THE ACTION IS TRANSITORY, AND VENUE IS PROPER IN SAN FRANCISCO COUNTY WHERE THE CONTRACT WAS EXECUTED. CCP SECTION 395. ALSO, THE VENUE SELECTION CLAUSE IS NOT VOID, BECAUSE IT DOES NOT DISRUPT STATUTORY VENUE PROVISIONS. (SEE ALEXANDER VS. SUPERIOR COURT (2003) 114 CAL.APP. 4TH 723.) TEN DAYS LEAVE TO ANSWER. COUNSEL WAIVE NOTICE OF RULING. NOTICE OF BANKRUPTCY STAY FILED BY DEFENDANT SAIGON VILLAGE LLC, AND THE RULING DOES NOT APPLY TO THIS DEFENDANT BECAUSE OF THE STAY FILED. JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797

Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Change Venue To Alameda County And Req. For Judicial Ntc
Off Calendar Dec-07-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANTS THOMAS NGUYEN, DIEP LE, and SAIGON VILLAGE, LLC Motion to Change Venue from San Francisco Superior Court to to Alameda County Superior Court, State of California. Appearances: Christopher H. Hart, Esq. of Schnader Attorneys at Law, (415) 364-6700 appearing for Plaintiff and opposing party; Lawrence A. Jacobson, Esq. of Cohen and Jacobson (650) 261-6280 appearing for Defendants and Moving party. Argued and the Court adopted and modified its tentative ruling as follows: Motion denied as to Defendants Thomas Nguyen and Diep Le only. The main relief sought is for recovery of the full amount owed under the loan agreement. The case arises from a underlying breach of contract. The action is transitory, and venue is proper in San Francisco County where the contract was executed. CCP section 395. Also, the venue selection clause is not void, because it does not disrupt statutory venue provisions. (See Alexander vs. Superior Court (2003) 114 Cal.App. 4th 723.) Ten days leave to answer. Counsel waive notice of ruling. Notice of Bankruptcy stay filed by Defendant Saigon Village LLC, and the ruling does not apply to this defendant because of the stay filed. (302/CWW)Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine CSR 5797

Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Change Venue To Alameda County And Req. For Judicial Ntc
